title: Raspberry almond traybake
subtitle: Frangipane-style almond cake with fresh raspberries
excerpt: An almond-rich traybake baked in one tin, with fresh raspberries pressed into the batter before it goes into the oven. The almond base stays dense and moist; the raspberries collapse and become jammy in the heat. Cut into squares. No frosting needed.
sourceNotes: Almond traybakes in the frangipane tradition are common across British and French home baking. The combination of ground almonds, butter, eggs, and sugar as a cake base appears throughout European baking as a simpler version of the classic almond cream used in tarts. The addition of fresh raspberries pressed into the surface before baking is a common British home adaptation.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool completely and freeze in a single layer. Wrap individual squares in baking paper and freeze in a box. The raspberry pieces will be slightly softer once thawed.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The traybake keeps well in an airtight tin at room temperature for 3 days. The almond crumb actually improves on the day after baking.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; bake low and slow so the almond base sets without drying out, 35 to 40 minutes

glossaryTerms.definition: A frangipane-style batter is done when the top is golden and the centre does not wobble when the tin is gently shaken. A skewer should come out with a few moist crumbs but no wet batter. The high almond content means the texture will always be slightly denser and more moist than a plain sponge.

content.text: This is an easy traybake that works because the
is very forgiving. Use the raspberries from fresh or frozen (straight from the freezer, unthawed, pressed in gently). Frozen berries may bleed more colour into the batter but taste equally good.

Heat the oven to 170 degrees C fan. Grease and line a 30x20 cm rectangular baking tin. Beat 200 g softened butter with 200 g caster sugar until pale and fluffy. Beat in 3 eggs one at a time. Add 1 tsp vanilla extract. Fold in 150 g almond flour and 100 g self-raising flour until just combined. Spread the batter into the prepared tin. Press 200 g raspberries evenly into the surface. Scatter 30 g flaked almonds over the top. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until the top is golden and a skewer comes out with a few moist crumbs. Cool in the tin for 20 minutes before lifting out on the paper and cutting into squares. Dust lightly with icing sugar if you like.

items.problem: Traybake is still wet in the centre after 40 minutes
items.solution: Ovens vary. Give it another 5 to 10 minutes and check again. A high raspberry content adds moisture; if the berries were frozen and released a lot of juice, the centre will take longer to set.

The batter was too thin, or the raspberries were too heavy. Toss fresh raspberries lightly in a teaspoon of flour before pressing in. For frozen berries, add them straight from the freezer while still firm.
